summary: Enhancement 6 — Identity & Security tested end-to-end. Backend (security_extras.py): all 13 pytest cases passed covering Monitored Accounts CRUD (create email/phone/ssn_last4, masking of sensitive identifiers, invalid type → 400, update, delete) plus Jurisdiction (initial fallback OK, after PUT /api/profile {home_county:'DeKalb', home_state:'GA'} the matched DeKalb County Police Department record is returned, and the dynamic /security/identity-theft/police-step and /security/identity-theft-guide police_report step both reflect DeKalb data). Frontend: /security/breach loads after login, MONITORED ACCOUNTS card + Add button present, EditModal opens with Account Type/Identifier/Label fields, full create→edit→delete cycle works, modal closes correctly and rows update in real time. HIBP key card and existing breach list still render. Minor non-blocking observations noted in action items (EditModal testID is rendered as 'undefined-*' because breach.tsx does not pass a testID prop to <EditModal>).
action_items: Optional polish: pass a testID prop to <EditModal> in /app/frontend/app/security/breach.tsx (e.g. testID="monitored-edit-modal") so child testIDs become 'monitored-edit-modal-identifier' etc. Currently they render as 'undefined-identifier', 'undefined-save', etc. Tests still work via these literal strings, but it's cleaner for future automation.

Console warnings (non-blocking, RN web): 'shadow* style props are deprecated. Use boxShadow.' and 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents.' — these come from upstream components/libraries; safe to ignore but worth tracking.
critical_code_review_comments: security_extras.py: masking logic correctly strips raw 'identifier' from list responses for phone/ssn_last4 (verified). Good.

Create endpoint catches DuplicateKeyError → 409, but no unique index is created on (user_id, identifier) — so duplicates currently succeed. If de-dup is desired, add an index in startup. Not a regression; works as documented today.
PUT /api/security/monitored-accounts/{id} accepts identifier change with same masking treatment on next GET — verified by test_06.
Jurisdiction lookup normalises ' County' suffix and lowercases — handles 'DeKalb', 'DeKalb County', 'dekalb' inputs correctly.

breach.tsx: row onPress passes m.identifier ?? m.masked_identifier as identifier into the edit modal. For phone/ssn_last4 (where raw identifier is stripped server-side), this means the user edits a masked value (e.g. '***-***-1234'); saving would persist the masked string as the new identifier. Consider either (a) not allowing edit of identifier for phone/ssn, or (b) returning identifier when editing. Low priority — does not break flow but is a UX/data-integrity concern.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests transiently create email/phone/ssn_last4 monitored accounts and clean them up. Profile updated to {home_county:'DeKalb', home_state:'GA'} for jurisdiction tests (not reverted — informational only).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_security_extras.py and run via `pytest tests/test_security_extras.py` from /app/backend. Test user (test1@plos.app/test123) now has home_county='DeKalb', home_state='GA' persisted in user_profile after this run — if a future test needs a different county, PUT /api/profile to override.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no issues found; all 13 backend tests and 7 frontend steps passed.
